what_it_does: A Workflow script (JS DSL with parallel/agent/phase/log primitives) that dispatches two file-disjoint build agents in parallel: lane G24 wires schema-guided decoding through fabric/transport.py to vLLM's guided_json so role-JSON cannot be malformed, and lane G14 adds a structured swap-approval response in ops/cli/capabilities.py so a model load needing eviction returns an approve-able ASK instead of hard-blocking or silently evicting. Build-only, no commit; each agent returns a schema-constrained report.
observations: Declares meta (name/description/phases) and a strict output SCHEMA (additionalProperties:false, required lane/files_written/how_verified/status, status enum built-verified|built-untested|blocked).

A LAWS preamble binds each agent: read AGENTS.md→MAP.md→STATE.md→module AGENTS.md + SYSTEM-GAPS.md first; LAW-0 UNIFICATION (finish, don't fork); reuse-don't-parallel; fail-loud; registry-is-truth; additive/schema-compatible; FLOOR STAYS; GPU-aware; no commit; no Gemini; no green-paint.
Explicitly fences territory: agents must NOT touch mcp_face/* or runtime/suite.py (the lead's serial work right now) — file-disjointness is the parallelism safety.

G24 carries concrete evidence: 3 files (runtime/bridge.py, roles/check.py, nodes/portal.py) deterministically fail the 4B digest with unparseable JSON; the fix wires the existing role output_schema through to guided decoding.
G24 instructs to FIND a possibly half-built json_schema transport lane and finish it rather than fork (unification law applied concretely).

G14 implements Tim's 2026-06-09 design: needs-eviction → return {swap_needed, would_load, would_evict, free_gb, needed_gb, approve:'re-call with ensure_evict=true'}, never hard-block/silent-evict; the agent/operator decides.
Both lanes carry verify-by-USE instructions (reproduce the failing digests; drive the needs-eviction branch with mocked VRAM, never actually evict resident chat-4b+embed-bge).
Cross-lane seam discipline: if runtime/cognition.py needs a pass-through change, report it as a NEEDED-SEAM, don't edit runtime/ (the lead applies it).
standouts: Guided-decoding as a determinism fix: making malformed role-JSON structurally impossible via vLLM guided_json rather than retry/repair.
The swap-approval ASK pattern: a resource-eviction decision surfaced as an approve-able structured response instead of an exception or silent action — governance applied to GPU memory.
GPU-aware guardrails ('check company gpu', never stomp the co-resident chat-4b+embed-bge) baked into the agent laws.
conventions: Follows the file-disjoint-parallel-lanes build pattern with a single bounded wave, schema-constrained agent reports, and per-lane report.json — the same protocol as the build-loop docs.
BUILD-ONLY no-commit (the loop's next beat verifies+commits), reuse-don't-parallel, fail-loud, additive/schema-compatible — repo laws restated.
Verify-by-USE with a named regression set rather than code-reading.
concerns: Hardcoded absolute paths in the LAWS string tie the script to /home/tim/company.
Agent success is inferred by built.filter(Boolean) — a returned object always truthy, so 'ok' counts presence of a report, not status==built-verified; the status check is left to the verifying beat.
notes: A two-lane parallel build workflow: lane G24 makes role-JSON unmalformable via vLLM guided decoding through fabric/transport.py; lane G14 turns a GPU eviction need into an approve-able swap ASK in capabilities.py — build-only, file-disjoint, verify-by-use.
questions: What are the Workflow/agent/parallel/phase/log primitives — where is this DSL runtime defined?
Did the L-transport lane's json_schema support actually exist to be finished, or did G24 have to build it?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — meta.description states G24 guided decoding ∥ G14 swap-approval, build+verify-by-use no commit, and the dispatched agent prompts implement exactly those two lanes with that discipline.
apparent_themes: Governance as approve-able ASK rather than hard-block or silent action
File-disjoint parallel lanes with fenced territory
Unification: finish half-built seams, don't fork
intention_signals: Trying to close two specific engine seams in parallel — eliminating malformed role-JSON at the decode layer and replacing GPU eviction's hard-fail/silent-evict with an operator-approvable swap response — under strict no-fork, no-commit, file-disjoint discipline.
novelty: Pairing a decode-time determinism fix (guided_json) with a memory-governance ASK in one parallel build wave, and the explicit 'report it as a NEEDED-SEAM, the lead applies it' protocol for cross-territory dependencies.
contribution: Contributes the concrete dispatch spec for two engine seams: schema-guided decoding wiring through fabric/transport.py and the structured swap-approval response shape in ops/cli/capabilities.py, plus the laws/schema/territory-fencing scaffold around them.
summary_for_embedding: A parallel build-workflow script dispatching two file-disjoint agents: one wiring vLLM guided-JSON decoding through fabric/transport.py, one adding a swap-approval ASK response for GPU eviction in ops/cli/capabilities.py.
intention_for_embedding: To make malformed role-JSON impossible via guided decoding and to surface GPU model-eviction as an approve-able request instead of a hard-block or silent evict, built in parallel without forking existing seams.
